verses.text: Leper's cleansing: priest goes outside the camp. If healed — two clean birds, cedar, scarlet, hyssop. One bird killed over running water in an earthen vessel. The living bird with cedar, scarlet, hyssop dipped in the blood and water; sprinkle the leper seven times; let the living bird loose. Leper washes, shaves, bathes — clean. Seven days outside. Day seven shaves all hair, bathes — clean. Guidance: One bird dies, one bird flies free. The picture is hard to miss: death and resurrection, the contagion taken away. Healing becomes liturgy.

Eighth day: two male lambs, one ewe, flour with oil, a log of oil. Priest presents the man. One lamb for trespass with the log — wave before the LORD. Trespass blood on the man's right ear, thumb, big toe. Oil in priest's left palm, sprinkle seven times. Oil on ear, thumb, toe over the blood. Rest on his head. Sin, burnt, meat — atone, clean. Guidance: The same ear-thumb-toe as Aaron's consecration. The healed leper is welcomed back like a priest — what he hears, does, walks is set apart anew.

If he is poor — one lamb for trespass, a tenth ephah flour, a log of oil; two doves or pigeons (one sin, one burnt). Eighth day, same procedure: trespass waved, blood on ear/thumb/toe, oil sprinkled seven times, oil on ear/thumb/toe and head. The two birds with the meat offering. The law for him who cannot afford the full set. Guidance: Even the poorest leper has a path home. God scales the ceremony to the wallet but never to the heart. The healing is real either way.

The LORD: when in Canaan I put leprosy in a house — owner tells the priest. Priest empties it first. Greenish or reddish streaks below the wall — shut seven days. Spread — break out stones, scrape, cast dust outside. Replace, plaster. If returns — tear down the house; carry stones and dust outside. Whoever enters unclean till evening; sleep or eat — wash clothes. Guidance: God's holiness reaches the walls of your home. A house can be sick; sometimes it has to be torn down. The cost of cleanness is sometimes a house.

If after replastering the plague does not return — clean. To cleanse: two birds, cedar, scarlet, hyssop; kill one over running water; dip the live bird, cedar, scarlet, hyssop in the blood; sprinkle the house seven times; let the bird loose. Atone for the house; clean. Guidance: The same two-bird ritual that cleansed the body cleanses the house. Walls get the gospel too. The contagion flies away into the field; the home returns to its place in God's camp.

This is the law of all manner of plague of leprosy and scall — of garment, of house, of a rising, scab, bright spot — to teach when unclean and when clean. This is the law of leprosy. Guidance: Body, garment, house — the law covers all three. Holiness shapes skin, cloth, walls. Nothing in God's people's lives is small enough to escape His care for purity.
